product_overview: Thrifty is a mobile subscription-warning app that alerts users before free trials convert or subscriptions renew, so they can cancel in time. It uses passwordless magic-link authentication and a Python FastAPI backend to persist subscriptions, compute alerts, and manage notification delivery across devices.
core_goals: Intercept subscription charges before money leaves the account by alerting users ahead of renewal or trial conversion events.
Provide passwordless, single-use magic-link authentication with session tokens and logout support.
Let users manually add subscriptions and keep records persistent across app deletion, offload, and device changes.
Normalise subscription data from manual entry and platform sync into one canonical server-side list.
Compute proactive alert timings and support alert recomputation for subscriptions.
Register and deactivate push notification tokens for iOS and Android.
Return honest, English subscription details without inventing unknown values.

features.name: Health Check API
features.description: Verifies that the API process and database connection are available.
user_flows: GET /health -> Receive 200 with status, db, and version fields showing the service is healthy
GET /health when the database session is unavailable -> Receive a non-200 error response indicating the backend or database health check failed

features.name: Passwordless Authentication API
features.description: Issues single-use magic links, verifies them into session tokens, and logs users out without passwords.
user_flows: POST /v1/auth/start with a valid email address -> Receive 202 accepted -> User receives a magic link -> POST /v1/auth/verify with the token -> Receive 200 with session_token and user profile
POST /v1/auth/start repeatedly for the same email beyond the rate limit -> Receive 429 too many links requested
POST /v1/auth/verify with an invalid or expired token -> Receive 401 link invalid or expired
POST /v1/auth/verify with a token that has already been used -> Receive 410 link already used
POST /v1/auth/logout with an active client session -> Receive 204 no content and the session ends

features.name: Subscription Management API
features.description: Creates, lists, updates, deletes, and platform-syncs subscription records while preserving unknown values honestly.
user_flows: POST /v1/auth/verify with a valid token -> Receive 200 session token -> POST /v1/subscriptions with service_name and optional manual fields -> Receive 201 with the created subscription -> GET /v1/subscriptions with the session token -> Receive 200 with the new subscription in the list
POST /v1/subscriptions with a valid session token but missing required service_name -> Receive 422 validation error
PATCH /v1/subscriptions/{subscription_id} with a valid session token and owned subscription changes -> Receive 200 with updated subscription data and recomputed alert fields
PATCH /v1/subscriptions/{subscription_id} for a subscription that does not exist or is not owned -> Receive 404 subscription not found
DELETE /v1/subscriptions/{subscription_id} with a valid session token -> Receive 204 no content -> GET /v1/subscriptions -> Receive 200 with the subscription removed from the active list

POST /v1/subscriptions/sync/platform with platform storekit and platform snapshots from a signed-in user -> Receive 200 with created, updated, and unchanged counts -> GET /v1/subscriptions -> Receive 200 with synced subscriptions visible
POST /v1/subscriptions/sync/platform with an invalid platform value or malformed snapshots -> Receive 422 validation error
GET /v1/subscriptions without a valid session token -> Receive 401 missing or invalid session token

features.name: Alert Scheduling API
features.description: Lists and recomputes proactive alerts for trial conversions and renewals before charges happen.
user_flows: POST /v1/auth/verify with a valid token -> Receive 200 session token -> GET /v1/alerts with the session token -> Receive 200 with scheduled alerts for the user
POST /v1/alerts/recompute with the session token and an optional subscription_id -> Receive 200 with alerts_created and alerts_cancelled counts reflecting the recomputed alert set
GET /v1/alerts with a status filter and from datetime filter -> Receive 200 with only matching alerts returned
GET /v1/alerts without a valid session token -> Receive 401 missing or invalid session token
POST /v1/alerts/recompute without a valid session token -> Receive 401 missing or invalid session token

features.name: Notification Token API
features.description: Registers and deactivates native push notification tokens for the signed-in user.
user_flows: POST /v1/auth/verify with a valid token -> Receive 200 session token -> POST /v1/notifications/token with platform ios and a device token -> Receive 201 with token id, platform, and active status
POST /v1/notifications/token with an invalid platform value or malformed token payload -> Receive 422 validation error
DELETE /v1/notifications/token/{token_id} with a valid session token and owned token -> Receive 204 no content -> The token is deactivated for future push delivery
DELETE /v1/notifications/token/{token_id} for a token that does not exist -> Receive 404 token not found
POST /v1/notifications/token without a valid session token -> Receive 401 missing or invalid session token

features.name: Health Check API
features.description: Verifies that the API process and database connection are available.

endpoints.description: Return API, database, and version status.

response_schema.200: { status: string, db: string, version: string }

features.name: Passwordless Authentication API
features.description: Issues single-use magic links, verifies them into session tokens, and logs out without passwords.

endpoints.description: Accept an email address and issue a single-use magic link.
Verify a magic-link token and return a session token plus user profile.

response_schema.200: { session_token: string, user: { id: string, email: string, tier: string } }

features.name: Subscription Management API
features.description: Creates, lists, updates, deletes, and platform-syncs subscription records while preserving unknown values honestly.

endpoints.description: List the signed-in user's subscriptions, optionally including deleted records.
Create a manual subscription and compute alerts for its next event.
Patch an owned subscription and recompute its alerts.
Soft delete an owned subscription and cancel pending alerts.
Upsert StoreKit or Play Billing snapshots by platform and source product id.

response_schema.200: { created: integer, updated: integer, unchanged: integer }

features.name: Alert Scheduling API
features.description: Lists and recomputes proactive alerts for trial conversions and renewals.

endpoints.description: List the signed-in user's alerts with optional status and lower-bound datetime filters.
Recompute alerts for all active subscriptions or one subscription id.

features.name: Notification Token API
features.description: Registers and deactivates native push notification tokens for the signed-in user.

endpoints.description: Register or reactivate a push notification token for iOS or Android.

response_schema.201: { id: string, platform: string, active: boolean }

endpoints.description: Deactivate an owned push notification token.

known_limitations.issue: Magic-link delivery is console or configured email dependent in local development.
known_limitations.impact: End-to-end auth verification may need direct test-token access or a configured email provider.

known_limitations.issue: StoreKit and Play Billing ingestion are represented by a backend sync contract only.
known_limitations.impact: Native platform purchase retrieval requires future iOS and Android clients.
